# Command Safety for Agents

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate_agent_assets.py; edit agent-command-policy.toml. -->

This page is generated from the canonical `agent-command-policy.toml` at the repository root. The published [machine-readable policy](agent-command-policy.json) is version-matched to the CLI. `llms.txt` is only a discovery map; this policy is the authorization contract.

## Authorization tiers

| Order | Tier | Rule |
| ---: | --- | --- |
| 1 | `automatic-read-only` | May run automatically when its independent sensitive-output guard is also satisfied. |
| 2 | `scoped-local-mutation` | Requires authorization for the named local write scope. |
| 3 | `explicit-runtime-or-external-mutation` | Requires explicit authorization before executing workload code or changing an external non-scheduler resource. |
| 4 | `explicit-quota` | Requires explicit authorization for scheduler submission, allocation, or quota consumption. |
| 5 | `explicit-destructive` | Requires explicit authorization for deletion, cancellation, requeue, or other destructive mutation. |

Use the highest applicable tier after applying every matching flag override. Approval for one command is scoped to that invocation; it does not authorize later submissions, cancels, deletions, SSH, or runtime execution.

## Sensitive output is independent

A command tagged `sensitive-output` needs a separate data-handling check even when it is read-only. Agents must not echo or ingest unredacted output from `plan --show-script`, `render`, `plan --verbose`, `--show-values`, `logs`, or `debug`. Prefer `hpc-compose --offline plan --format json` and `hpc-compose --offline explain --format json`. When a user explicitly requests a script file, write it to an owner-only destination and do not read it back into the conversation.

Global `--offline` removes network/SSH and scheduler effects, but it does not make local writes, deletes, runtime execution, or sensitive output safe automatically.

## Effect tags

| Tag | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| `local-read` | Reads local files or process metadata without changing them. |
| `local-write` | Creates or changes local files, tracked records, caches, or generated artifacts. |
| `local-delete` | Deletes local files, cache entries, tracked state, or workspace data. |
| `executes-user-code` | Runs workload commands, preparation commands, probes, or an interactive runtime. |
| `network-or-ssh` | Uses the network, pulls remote content, opens a tunnel, or delegates over SSH. |
| `scheduler-read` | Queries Slurm or another external scheduler without changing scheduler state. |
| `scheduler-submit` | Submits a job or opens an allocation and may consume quota. |
| `scheduler-cancel` | Cancels, signals, requeues, or otherwise destructively changes a scheduled job. |
| `polls` | Repeats local, network, or scheduler observations until completion or interruption. |
| `sensitive-output` | May expose secrets, commands, scripts, paths, logs, tokens, or other sensitive material. |

## Complete command classification

| Command path | Base tier | Base effects | Flag-sensitive overrides |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `hpc-compose alloc` | `explicit-quota` | `local-read`, `local-write`, `executes-user-code`, `network-or-ssh`, `scheduler-read`, `scheduler-submit`, `polls`, `sensitive-output` | — |
| `hpc-compose artifacts` | `scoped-local-mutation` | `local-read`, `local-write` | — |
| `hpc-compose cache` | `automatic-read-only` | None | — |
| `hpc-compose cache inspect` | `automatic-read-only` | `local-read` | — |
| `hpc-compose cache list` | `automatic-read-only` | `local-read` | — |
| `hpc-compose cache prune` | `explicit-destructive` | `local-read`, `local-write`, `local-delete` | — |
| `hpc-compose cancel` | `explicit-destructive` | `local-read`, `local-write`, `local-delete`, `scheduler-cancel` | — |
| `hpc-compose checkpoints` | `automatic-read-only` | `local-read` | — |
| `hpc-compose clean` | `explicit-destructive` | `local-read`, `local-write`, `local-delete` | `--dry-run`: tier `automatic-read-only`, removes `local-write`, `local-delete` |
| `hpc-compose completions` | `automatic-read-only` | `local-read` | — |
| `hpc-compose config` | `automatic-read-only` | `local-read` | `--show-values`: adds `sensitive-output` |
| `hpc-compose context` | `automatic-read-only` | `local-read` | `--show-values`: adds `sensitive-output` |
| `hpc-compose debug` | `automatic-read-only` | `local-read`, `scheduler-read`, `sensitive-output` | — |
| `hpc-compose dev` | `explicit-runtime-or-external-mutation` | `local-read`, `local-write`, `executes-user-code`, `network-or-ssh`, `polls`, `sensitive-output` | — |
| `hpc-compose diff` | `automatic-read-only` | `local-read`, `scheduler-read` | — |
| `hpc-compose docs` | `automatic-read-only` | `local-read` | — |

| `hpc-compose workspace extend` | `explicit-runtime-or-external-mutation` | `local-read`, `local-write`, `executes-user-code` | — |
| `hpc-compose workspace release` | `explicit-destructive` | `local-read`, `local-write`, `local-delete`, `executes-user-code` | — |
| `hpc-compose workspace status` | `scoped-local-mutation` | `local-read`, `local-write`, `executes-user-code` | — |

## High-risk examples

- `when` and `germinate` submit automatically after their conditions or canary setup; only `germinate --dry-run` removes submission effects.
- `preflight --fs-probes`, `doctor mpi-smoke --submit`, and `doctor fabric-smoke --submit` consume a small allocation.
- `test --preemption` submits, signals, and requeues a job and therefore uses the destructive tier.
- `sweep observe --watch --stop-when` and `sweep stop` can cancel trials; `sweep submit --resume` can submit previously unattempted trials.
- `workspace allocate` and `workspace extend` mutate external site storage; `workspace release`, cache pruning, rendezvous pruning, cleanup, and cancellation are destructive.
- Local runtime modes (`up --local`, `run --local`, `notebook --local`, `dev`, `tmux`, and `test --local`) execute workload code and are never automatic read-only operations.
